title: Technical Architect
description: **Role: Senor Solutions Architect** We are looking for a talented senior Solutions Architect to work in the Risk Technology team. The candidate should be interested in designing mission critical applications within one of the most interesting parts of one of the world’s largest banks. **As a Solutions Architect your main responsibilities will involve:** * Responsible for designing and documenting solutions to help solve complex business problems * Define and enforce architecture standards, coding best practices, and DevOps/CI\-CD pipelines. * Hands\-on development of PoCs/R\&D initiatives to validate technical feasibility and drive innovation. * Partner with risk, compliance, and engineering teams to translate business needs into technical solutions. * Create UML diagrams, Visio workflows, and clear technical documentation for diverse stakeholders (executives, developers, auditors). * Advise on technology selection, vendor evaluations, and cost\-benefit analyses for risk systems. * Mentor engineering teams on risk\-aware design patterns, cloud security, and resilient architecture. **As Solutions Architect your skills and qualifications will ideally include:** * 5\+ years in solution architecture or equivalent roles * Expertise in microservices/SOA design using containerized environments * Experience with high\-volume ETL/data processing * Experience designing polyglot systems using different database technologies – RDBMS (SQL Server or PostgreSQL), NOSQL (ideally MongoDB) * Experience with RESTful/GraphQL web APIs and async communication flows using (Redis Streams, Kafka etc.) * Exceptional stakeholder management, diagramming (UML/Visio), and presentation skills. Ability to simplify complex technical concepts for non\-technical audiences * Strong written and oral communication skills **Bonus skills** * Experience with TimeSeries databases (like Prometheus), Graph native databases like Neo4j or TigerGraph etc. * Hands on coding skills in Python * Familiarity with Agile methodology

title: Technical Support Engineer
description: **Who is Tenable?** Tenable® is the Exposure Management company. 44,000 organizations around the globe rely on Tenable to understand and reduce cyber risk. Our global employees support 65 percent of the Fortune 500, 45 percent of the Global 2000, and large government agencies. Come be part of our journey! **What makes Tenable such a great place to work?** Ask a member of our team and they’ll answer, “Our people!” We work together to build and innovate best\-in\-class cybersecurity solutions for our customers; all while creating a culture of belonging, respect, and excellence where we can be our best selves. When you’re part of our \#OneTenable team, you can expect to partner with some of the most talented and passionate people in the industry, and have the support and resources you need to do work that truly matters. We deliver results that exceed expectations and we win together! **Your Role** Join our Technical Support Team at Tenable where you’ll play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between our products and customers. Solve real\-world technical challenges by offering exceptional support for cutting\-edge vulnerability assessment and compliance auditing software. Your expertise will shine in complex, multi\-layered customer environments. Our suite of products, detailed at https://www.tenable.com/products, offers a diverse range of opportunities. From assisting customers with their first Nessus scanner to guiding them through intricate log correlation and real\-time vulnerability analysis, you'll contribute to mitigating cyber risks and closing the Cyber Exposure gap. Join us in delivering top\-notch support that makes a difference! **Your Opportunity** * Serves as the primary technical liaison between the customer and Tenable internal groups (Product Management, Sales, R\&D, etc) for technical issues. * Analyse and clarify customer technical inquiries. * Analysing vulnerability scan results, system audits, and log events. * Recreating customer software issues in a lab environment. * Harness AI\-driven technologies to amplify your impact, automate routine tasks, and focus on solving complex technical challenges. * Maintaining in\-depth knowledge of Tenable products and information security best\-practices. * Create and publish solution knowledge for re\-use by customers and Tenable employees. **What You'll Need** * Bachelor’s degree in IT or Computer Science (or equivalent experience). * 2\+ years professional experience with networking (TCP/IP), OS administration(Linux/Windows/MacOS), patch deployment, and system configuration. * Experience providing technical support to enterprise clients, troubleshooting complex issues in production or customer environments; network security experience a plus. * Familiarity with Artificial Intelligence (AI) as Tenable continues to build an AI\-augmented support model to accelerate case resolutions and to provide an exceptional customer/TSE experience. * Knowledge of computer, network, and application security; familiarity with Nessus or similar tools is a bonus. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to learn quickly. * Excellent written and verbal communication; customer\-focused mindset. * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment to help meet business and client needs. * Monthly availability to work weekends (8am\-4:30pm) and Holidays is also required. **And Ideally** * Using Nessus and/or other vulnerability management or cybersecurity tools, either professionally or in an academic setting. * Experience with ticketing systems (JIRA, Salesforce, Zendesk etc). * Log analysis using a SEIM product (Splunk, ElasticSearch, etc). * Database technologies and SQL knowledge (Oracle, MySQL, MSSQL, etc). * Cloud/Virtualization technologies (AWS, Azure, Docker, Kubernetes, etc). * Fundamental understanding of programming languages. * Experience with or understanding of Certificates and CA’s. * Basic understanding of reviewing PCAPs with tools such as Wireshark. * Bilingual candidates are desirable but not required (Arabic/French/German/Spanish/Italian). *We’re committed to promoting Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) at Tenable \- through all equal employment opportunity laws and regulations at the international, federal, state and local levels. If you need a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruiting process, please contact* *Recruiting@Tenable.com* *for further assistance.* **Tenable Data Consent Statement** Tenable is committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal data. This Notice describes how we collect and use your personal data during and after your working relationship with us, in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (“GDPR”). Please click here to review. For California Residents: The California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) requires that Tenable advise you of certain rights related to the collection of your private information. Please click here to review.

title: Telecoms Engineer - Data Center SME
description: **Arcadis are seeking a Telecoms Design Engineer at SME level to work on Datacenter projects with a Big Tech client in Dublin.** **Position Summary** Datacenter Design Subject Matter Expert will manage, review, coordinate and provide key technical input during site due diligence, detailed design and/or construction administration phases of a datacenter project. This position will collaborate and manage internal and external resources for the delivery of discipline specific systems aligning with client and site\-specific requirements. **Job Description** The Datacenter Design Subject Matter Expert will be responsible for the facilitation and delivery of industry\-leading datacenter designs including the development of new technology and advanced concepts from pre\-programming through design development, construction, and the turnover to Operations. This role will provide technical direction, decision\-making, document review and overall leadership for our datacenter architectural / engineering designs. To be successful in this role the candidate will oversee the delivery of multiple, concurrent, complete Datacenter design solutions aligned with the scopes, schedules, and budgets of the projects. The Design Subject Matter Expert will engage with key groups internally as well as outside vendors who specialize in datacenter architecture and engineering. **Knowledge, Experience and Skills:** Successful candidates will lead and direct architecture or engineering design consultants in the design and coordination of discipline specific systems. The candidate must have excellent leadership, communication, and presentation skills. Candidate must possess a high level of proficiency in their specific discipline with a primary focus on datacenter design. The candidate must also be able to work with other technical and non\-technical groups in a fast\- paced environment and do so with an even temperament. The candidate must be a person of integrity, be methodical, detailed\- oriented with a strong work ethic. * Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering or Electrical Engineering Technology. * Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering in related field * Professional Engineer * Minimum of 10 years’ experience in consulting engineering * Minimum of 5 years’ experience as lead engineer, lead architect or design manager * Candidate must possess strong written and verbal communication skills * Ability to lead teams of engineers in advanced engineering and operational processes and procedures as they relate to large datacenter environments. * Ability to manage, prioritize and successfully execute multiple concurrent projects. **Interested parties should forward a copy of their CV through the link below or contact Fergal Walshe via LinkedIn for further information**

title: Telecoms OSP Engineer
description: **Company Description** Novegen is a trusted leader in Telecommunications Infrastructure Management and Independent Certification Solutions. We provide a wide range of services, including telecom asset mapping, network data management, open access network management, and fibre network testing. We help organisations optimise and scale their connectivity infrastructure. Committed to innovation, Novegen partners with clients to build the future of connectivity together. **Role Description** If you’re looking for the opportunity to flex your creative skills within your Engineering qualification, Novegen is providing the chance for you to not only take responsibility for project development with our Engineering team, but also work with internal stakeholders to design and build carrier\-class, access and last\-mile network infrastructure. Rather than just inputting data, a driven, skilled engineer can propose, develop, and implement new automated systems and processes within the project team. **What will you be doing?** * The successful graduate will work closely with the Engineering and GIS Managers who will provide them with regular and objective feedback, valuable insight and support. * Working with internal \& external design \& construction teams to design, deploy, QC \& record a carrier class network infrastructure. * You will need to be proactive and precise with the completion of project documentation. * Offering your advice and guidance to the project team along with data input onto the GIS system. * Be a member of our QA/QC team in relation to network certification. * Preparation and review of preliminary designs and reports using a variety of computer packages and subsequent preparation of detailed design drawings and specifications. * Preparation of Preliminary and Contract Drawings using a range of packages such as AutoCAD 2D/3D and ArcGIS. * Procurement of Site Investigation, Civil Engineering Works and technical contracts including preparation of pricing schedules and Bills of Quantities. * Administration of the construction contracts to ensure that design requirements, project schedules and project budget are tracked and monitored. **What tools will you be using to do this?** * AutoCAD, GIS, DXF, KMZ, KML, MapInfo, Fibre Mapping, Network Planning, Network Design, GIS Diagrams, Fibre Network Schematics. Full Microsoft \& Google suite. **We would like you to bring us:** * GIS/CAD experience within network planning environment \- GIS Diagrams and Fibre Network Schematics. * Understanding of civil infrastructure for telecoms. * 2\-3 years of practical experience. * Field experience. * Network Planning, Network Design. * Great communication skills are also necessary as you will be working closely with internal \& external multifunctional teams. * Previous experience with large networks and telecoms carriers will be advantageous. **Advantageous Skills and Experience:** * Excellent written and verbal communication. * AutoCAD 2D and 3D will be a basic requirement. * GIS skills. * Graduate of an Engineering discipline \- Civil/ Quantity surveying / Telecom, or related fields.

title: Town Planning Professional – CSR (Dublin & Cork)
description: Opportunity for Experienced Planning Professionals CSR is an established, award\-winning multi‑disciplinary consultancy delivering high‑quality planning, design, and construction services across the built environment. Driven by a strong commitment to excellence, the practice continues to grow its portfolio of public and private sector clients. We are currently seeking Town Planning Professionals to join our Dublin or Cork offices. The role offers exposure to a broad range of projects, including large‑scale residential schemes, commercial and retail development, urban regeneration, tourism, master planning, infrastructure, and environmental initiatives. The successful candidate will work on varied and rewarding projects within a collaborative, multi‑disciplinary team, with clear opportunities for professional development and role progression aligned to their skills and experience. **Role responsibilities** * Managing projects end to end, working closely with clients, stakeholders, and internal teams. * Developing project plans, overseeing budgets, and ensuring the timely delivery of high‑quality services. * Ensuring efficient delivery of client instructions. * Coordinating with statutory authorities, including County Councils and An Bord Pleanála. * Designing and implementing strategic responses to new legislation and policy changes. * Acting as a company representative at client meetings and across social media to support business development. **Ideal Candidate** * Professionals with 5\+ years planning and development experience. * Accredited planning qualification with IPI and/or RTPI membership, or equivalent. * Candidates should have 5\+ years of planning and development experience. * They should be strong communicators with excellent report\-writing skills, commercial awareness, the ability to work independently and collaboratively, and proven leadership skills. * Expert knowledge of Irish planning legislation and regulations. * Proven expertise in development planning, feasibility, environmental planning, and planning applications, appeals, and reports. * Strong analytical and decision\-making skills in complex planning matters. * Strong communicator and collaborative team member with a client\-focused approach. * Support junior team members and collaborate with our design, master\-planning and GIS teams * Excellent written, verbal, presentation, and organisational skills. **Salary and Benefits** Salary is negotiable and will be commensurate with skills and experience. CSR supports Professional Membership and Continuing Professional Development (CPD) as essential aspects of this role. Our range of benefits is tailored to meet individual needs. CSR is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace, offering flexible arrangements and supporting a healthy work\-life balance. This is a full\-time position based in our Dublin or Cork office. Candidate must be eligible to work in Ireland. **How to Apply** Candidates interested in the position should apply by emailing an up\-to\-date Curriculum Vitae and Cover Letter/Email to **info@csrlandplan.ie** Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company pension Application question(s): * Do you have knowledge and/or experience of Irish planning policy and legislation? Work authorisation: * Ireland (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Trading Operations Specialist - Digital Assets (EMEA Remote)
description: **About Keyrock** Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change\-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation. Today, we rock with over 220 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 42 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging from DeFi natives to PhDs. Predominantly remote, we have hubs in London, Brussels and Singapore, and host regular online and offline hangouts to keep the crew tight. We are trading on more than 80 exchanges, and working with a wide array of asset issuers. As a well\-established market maker, our distinctive expertise led us to expand rapidly. Today, our services span market making, options trading, high\-frequency trading, OTC, and DeFi trading desks. But we’re more than a service provider. We’re an initiator. We're pioneers in adopting the Rust Development language for our algorithmic trading, and champions of its use in the industry. We support the growth of Web3 startups through our Accelerator Program. We upgrade ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives. At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it. **What we are looking for…** a dedicated, EMEA based **Trading Operations Specialist** , to become a core part of the EMEA based trading operations team \- dedicated to working closely with our trading, treasury and wider operations teams during EMEA trading hours. **Job Description** * Managing digital asset and fiat related trade settlement and communicating with internal and external trading counterparties to ensure timely and accurate settlements. * Trade reconciliation from post trade to settlement to ensure correct and timely settlement. * Keeping track of the treasury, and liaising with the trading and finance teams on accounting for asset balances across operations. * Liaising internally with the wider trading, OTC and options teams on balances and settlement status, and providing administrative and client communication support where needed. * Asset rebalancing and whitelisting of crypto addresses across multiple CEXs and DEXs; across multiple platforms. * Managing and adding new banking beneficiaries, and supporting trading desks with fiat transfers and settlements. Liaising with Keyrock’s account managers at banks on confirming cut\-off times, fees and any troubleshooting of banking services. * Tracking and alerting internal and external client margin and net open positions, and liaising with clients on transfer of assets to maintain margin thresholds * Keeping track of and reporting technical and financial problems and providing support to the dev team for improvements and automating processes. * Monitoring and project managing internal operations ticketing processes and working with trading teams to streamline and improve these. * Overseeing and helping enhance the collection, documentation and organising of external counterparty FX and OTC related trade data for purposes of internal cost and best execution tracking. * Helping improve the efficiency and design of a more streamlined trade reporting and post\-trade settlement procedures. **Background And Experience** * 3\+ years of prior trading and/or trading operations experience \- with settlements and trading reconciliation at its core, and a strong preference if that included crypto assets. * Experience working in a crypto or tradfi banking environment across multiple currencies and managing different forms of money transfers/wires – with experience in managing clearing/settlement within cutoff times. * Minimum Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field e.g., Finance, Economics, Engineering, Accounting, etc. **Competences and personality** * Extremely strong attention to detail * Self\-organized and excellent communication * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities * A demonstrable passion for digital assets and blockchain technologies, and an understanding of asset transfers across CEXes and DEXes * Adaptable and a strong team player, whilst working in a fast paced environment * Fluent in English * Strong Excel skills and ability to reconcile, track and present asset treasury and balances to internal and external counterparties. * Added bonus: Familiarity with Python and SQL languages; and experience with querying database trade data and generating reports and dashboards. **Our recruitment philosophy** We value self\-awareness in our recruitment process. We seek people who understand themselves and their career goals. We're after those with the right skills and a conscious choice to join our field. The perfect fit? A crypto enthusiast who’s driven, collaborative, and delivers solid, scalable outcomes. **Our offer** * A competitive salary package with various benefits * The freedom to create your own entrepreneurial experience by being part of a team of people in search of excellence As an employer we are committed to building a positive and collaborative work environment. We hire, reward and promote entirely based on merit and performance. Due to the nature of our business and external requirements, we perform background checks on all potential employees, passing which is a prerequisite to join Keyrock. https://keyrock.com/careers/

title: Training Specialist
description: **DON’T BE IN THE DARK ABOUT YOUR CAREER…….** **WE WANT YOU TO HELP US CREATE THE DIGITAL FUTURE OF OUR COMPANY** We are looking for exceptional people to join our diverse and multi\-cultural team. This is a fantastic opportunity to play a lead role as we connect process, data and technology, creating sustainable business benefits and drive the achievement of Divisional, Sales Area and Common Function business outcomes. As we are creating a new operating model, this presents a great opportunity for learning and career development in this full\-time position. **We are now recruiting for a Training Specialist – (Hybrid), a permanent role, based in Dublin, Ireland.** This is a role reporting to the Head of Change Management and Training. The organization seeks to drive performance excellence through process improvement and delivering on desired business outcomes across all E2E processes. **Job Purpose** The Training Specialist is a key role within the Change Management and Training team, supporting global process, system, AI, and digital transformation initiatives through role\-based learning and enablement activities. Responsibilities include supporting the design, development, coordination, and continuous improvement of learning solutions aligned to approved business processes and ways of working. Working closely with the Training Lead, Change Management Lead and process teams, the Training Specialist helps employees build confidence in new processes, systems, and ways of working to support operational readiness and successful adoption. This role contributes to business adoption through practical, learner\-focused enablement activities and learning support across transformation and continuous improvement initiatives. **Main Responsibilities** * Design and develop enablement materials: Create role\-based learning and support materials including e\-learning, job aids, workflow guides, facilitator guides, and learner support materials based on approved business processes and aligned to agreed learning standards and governance requirements. * Plan and coordinate learning interventions: Coordinate training schedules, logistics, communications, and learner administration for virtual and instructor\-led sessions. Support instructors and delivery teams to ensure learning activities run smoothly across global audiences. * Support training needs analysis and planning: Work with process teams and the Training Lead to support the identification of role\-based learning needs and recommended enablement approaches. * Support super\-user enablement activities: Assist with onboarding materials, communications, and learning support activities for super\-users and local business support networks. * Contribute to readiness and adoption activities: Support learning readiness activities, track training and adoption metrics, and help identify improvement actions where required. * Collaborate across process and program teams: Partner with process teams and subject matter experts to develop practical learning materials and support a consistent user adoption approach. * Other Ad hoc Duties as requested by management. **Experience** * Experience supporting learning and enablement activities within transformation or technology implementation environments. * Experience developing digital learning, enablement, or communication materials. * Strong interpersonal and stakeholder coordination skills across global teams. * Ability to create visually clear and user\-friendly learning and communication materials. * Experience supporting training needs analysis and role\-based learning planning. * Organized and structured approach to managing priorities and stakeholder relationships. * Comfortable working across cultures, time zones, and fast\-paced delivery environments. Essential: * Minimum 3 years’ experience supporting learning or training activities within transformation or technology\-enabled environments. * Understanding of instructional design principles and learner\-focused content development. * Experience developing learner\-focused training and enablement materials. * Experience supporting training needs analysis and role\-based learning planning. * Strong written communication skills with the ability to produce clear and audience\-appropriate materials. Desirable: * Exposure to instructional design methodologies or certifications (e.g. ADDIE, SAM, ATD, CIPD, or equivalent). * Experience working within an ERP or enterprise systems transformation environment (SAP, Oracle, or equivalent). * Familiarity with simulation or digital adoption tools (e.g. SAP Enable Now, ClickLearn, WalkMe, or equivalent). * Experience deploying SCORM\-compliant learning content to an LMS (e.g. Workday Learning, Cornerstone, or equivalent). * Experience using data analysis and reporting tools such as Excel or Power BI to support learning insights and adoption reporting. * Experience using visual communication or content creation tools (e.g. Canva, Adobe Express, PowerPoint, or similar). Other: * Occasional travel may be required. **What we offer:** * 22 days annual leave * 4 Company Days * Defined Contribution Pension * Onsite gym * VHI Health Insurance * Life \& Disability Insurance * Health \& Wellbeing Initiatives – onsite massage therapist, gym membership, wellbeing seminars, bike to work scheme, etc * Flexible start \& Finish times * Hybrid working **Location** : Dublin, Ireland (Dockline, Mayor Street, IFSC, Dublin 1\) **Reporting to** : Head of Training and Change Management. **Diversity, Inclusion \& Sustainability** Sandvik has a developed belief in conducting our business in a sustainable and responsible manner, both for our employees and the environment; wherever in the world we operate. Our high ethical standards determine our policy of putting Safety at a premium, acknowledging and respecting the communities and giving due consideration to the environment in the areas we operate. These core values, to prioritise the safety and well\-being of our employees, are the foundation of how we work today. We also recognise the importance on inter\-personal relationships and how those based on honesty, respect and trust, enhance our working lives and help each other’s growth and career development. Equality of opportunity is fully supported and endorsed as an employer by Sandvik, while also welcoming the additional strength that diversity brings, by providing a work environment where everyone is included, treated fairly and with respect. **Sandvik Mining and Rock Solutions** Mining is a business area within the Sandvik Group and a global leading supplier of equipment and tools, parts, service, digital solutions and sustainability\-driving technologies for the mining and construction industries. Application areas include rock drilling, rock cutting, loading and hauling, tunneling and quarrying. In 2024, sales were approximately 64 billion SEK with about 17,000 employees. **A glimpse of our culture** At Sandvik, we’re tech driven, innovative and entrepreneurial! For us, success is a team effort \- we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where people can be themselves and reach their full potential. So, we invest in supporting each other, learning together and celebrating our differences. We also give you the freedom to find that perfect mix between work, family life and interests. Visit our stories hub, LinkedIn or Facebook to get to know us further. **How to apply** ? Interested in the challenge? Please apply via this advert or directly at https://www.home.sandvik/career Prior to this recruitment, we have already decided which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to utilise, and we respectfully decline any contact from marketing or recruitment agencies regarding additional channels or campaigns. **The closing date for applications is 25th May 2026** **Applicants must be eligible to work in the EU or hold a valid permit to work in the EU**

title: Travel Product Executive – Full time / permanent role.
description: Travel Department is Ireland’s leading escorted tour operator with an approach to business that is innovative, dynamic and professional. We have an unsurpassed reputation within our market for excellent customer service, quality holidays and value. For over 28 years, our team of experts have been creating fully guided group holidays with best\-in\-class service and an ever\-growing product portfolio. We have an exciting opportunity for a Product Executive. Are you a person with a flair for building travel itineraries and a passion for travel? If so, we just might have the perfect role for you! Our Product Executives work closely with our suppliers throughout the world to build and plan the best itineraries to suit our very valued customers. Do you have an outgoing personality with a commercial focus and strong negotiation skills? Previous travel industry experience is preferable. You would report directly to our Product Manager. **You will be responsible for:** * Travel Department products. * Booking and administering services with inbound operators, hotels, coach companies and guides. * Creating website content for all elements of the tour including itinerary and hotel descriptions. * Updating Travel Department’s website with product information with relevant SEO content. * Proofing and checking reader offers, newspaper inserts, brochures and other marketing material. * Providing appropriate reports to suppliers based on allocations and customer feedback. * Monitoring success of tours and responding effectively. * Assisting with developing new tour types and new destinations. * Updating / training reservations staff with product details. * Researching and developing new and interesting itineraries including new coaching itineraries and holidays to new destinations. * Occasional site visits for hotel \& destination inspections. * Review weekly trading reports and analyze data to report back to management with suggested actions. * Collaborating closely with all teams across the company to achieve the goal of TD successfully living its values. **We would like you to have:** * Very strong attention to detail. * Team working skills. * A commercial focus and strong negotiation skills. * An outgoing personality with the ability to manage relationships with people and suppliers worldwide. * The ability to learn quickly in a busy environment. * Flexibility \- ability to multitask. * Work well under pressure and to tight deadlines. * An excellent knowledge of Word / Excel. **Salary / package details for the right candidate:** * Full\-time permanent role. * Working days, Monday to Friday. * 20 days annual leave per year. * Position based in Dublin, served by many transport links with flexibility for a blend of home and office working. * Dynamic and exciting industry and work environment. If you would like to apply for this role, please email a short cover letter along with your CV to our Product Manager, Francesca Rizzarello at francesca@traveldepartment.ie The closing date for applications is 28/05/2026\. Only applicants who are selected for interview will be contacted. Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * Sick pay Application question(s): * What are your salary expectations for this role? * Do you live in Dublin / within a close commute of Dublin? Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin, County Dublin

title: Vending Machine Merchandiser & Delivery Driver
description: BDS Vending Solutions, part of Coca\-Cola HBC group, is expanding and looking for reliable, customer\-focused drivers to join our Dublin team! This role combines Delivery Driving \& Vending Machine Merchandising, giving you a varied workday across customer sites nationwide. We provide full training, set shift pattern, and the opportunity to build a long\-term career with a growing company. **Available Shifts:** We currently have several full\-time shift options (40 or 45 hours per week): * **Monday – Friday:** 4am\-1:30pm * **Monday – Friday:** 6:30am\-3pm * **Tuesday \- Saturday:** 6:30am\-3pm * **Sunday \- Thursday:** 6:30am\-3pm Overtime opportunities are also available! **Work Location:** On the road **Head Office Location:** Parkwest, Dublin 12 **Pay:** Starting Annual Salary **€32,219\.20 to €36,246\.60** (depending on hours worked) **guaranteed increase to €33,185\.78 to €37,334 after 6mths** (upon completion of probation). **Job Type:** Full\-time \| Permanent **What You’ll Do** * Deliver stock to customer locations and team members across Ireland * Restock, clean, merchandise, and rotate products in vending machines * Maintain equipment and ensure machines are operating correctly * Provide excellent customer service at every site * Report machine issues or stock shortages to our support team * Maintain a professional appearance and safe working practices **What We’re Looking For** * Full, clean B Manual Driving Licence (required) * A customer\-focused attitude * Comfortable driving a Van and lifting shallow crates of snacks and drinks (weighting max 17kg) * Good conversational English and communication skills * Strong time management and reliability * Motivation to work independently and deliver excellent results No prior vending or delivery experience? No problem. Full training will be provided. **What we Offer** * Fulltime hours * Regular set shift * Permanent position with job security * 20 days annual leave * Full uniform and PPE provided * Comprehensive training * Employee Assistance Programme * Opportunities for overtime and career development * A supportive and inclusive workplace culture Pay: €32,219\.20\-€36,246\.60 per year Benefits: * Company events * Employee assistance program * Free or subsidised travel * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Warehouse Operative (with some van driver duties)
description: Select Technology Group has 21 Apple stores throughout the UK and Ireland and DID is also part of our Group. What do you get from working at Select? * Competitive wage \- (will depend on experience) * A growing love and passion for all things Apple * Fantastic Employee discounts * Employee Incentives * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) * Cycle to Work Scheme * Recognition of long service We are looking for a Warehouse Operative to work as part of our warehouse and logistics team based in our Ballymount HQ and relocating later this year to Fonthill, Dublin 22\. You will assist with day\-to\-day stock management within the warehouse and to support the sales, retail, logistics and management teams. There will be requirement for some limited deliveries in the company van and, as a consequence of our van insurance terms, the candidate will need to be **aged 23 or over**. **Key responsibilities include** (but are not limited to): Working in collaboration with department teams, stock administrator(s), retail \& warehouse teams, on a daily basis **Warehouse Operative** Loading and unloading delivery vehicles, by hand or with a forklift truck \- mixed weights Checking for damaged or missing items and then sorting stock for storage in the warehouse Receiving and processing warehouse stock products (pick, unload, label, store) checking against paperwork and using the warehouse management systems. Reporting of issues and discrepancies Preparing and completing orders for delivery or pickup according to schedule (load, pack, wrap, label, ship) Picking orders, packing (correctly and safely with adequate packaging) and completing transfers on the company system as well as arranging shipping and loading goods on to delivery vehicles. Operating a forklift truck to move and rearrange stock and ensuring that products are stored safely and labelled correctly and safely Performing inventory controls and keep quality standards high for audits. Stock checks and stock takes as required Other warehouse operations, as required **General** Following quality service standards and complying with procedures, rules and regulations Keeping a clean and safe working environment and optimising space utilization. Cleaning and maintaining the warehouse is part of the daily routine. Communicating and co\-operating with supervisors and co\-workers Operating and maintaining preventively warehouse vehicles and equipment Comply with company and legislative Environment, Health and Safety requirements Completing a limited number of deliveries to customers and stores (currently up to ten local deliveries per week, subject to change) ### **Skills \& Competencies Required** Ability to prioritize and proactively manage workload, and solve problems Good time management and organizational skills Excellent attention to detail, with good communication skills 2\+ years' experience as a warehouse operative Ability to lift heavy objects Use of email is a requirement, knowledge of Microsoft Excel or Numbers a distinct advantage Leaving cert or equivalent level of education **Full clean driving licence (class B)** ### **Preferred Additional Skills** Current forklift licence Experience with SAP ### **Working Conditions** Work in a warehouse environment. Manual handling required. Use of the company van for deliveries and collections, as required. Occasional travel between offices/locations may be required. Please submit a CV for this full\-time position. **IND1**

title: early years relief person
description: Would you like to be part of a wonderful team who are passionate about learning and care, then we would like to hear from you...... Little Dale Academy are looking to expand their team We are looking to recruit a new fun upbeat, energetic Teacher who loves creating wonderful learning experiences for children. You will be joining a well established service with a great team. The role is a floating role which will see you assist all classes throughout the service where ever needed no experience is necessary but **qualifications in childcare** are manditory Rate of pay begins at €16\.50 for level5/6 and €17\.50 level 7\+wages are negotiable based on experience Start date is August 10th this is a fulltime role, 40 hours per week This role is a great opperunity to gain experience with all ages we are located in kilmacud close to stillorgan shopping centre and just beside the kilmacud /sandyford luas, our eircode is A94K8W7 Little Dale Academy is a family run creche operating for over 20 years in Kilmacud/Stillorgan. Working as part of our team, we have the benefit of small class sizes ranging between 10 and 17 children , all classes are operate in ratio with 2\-3 Teachers in each Start date is as soon as possible **Role/Duties:** \- To provide quality early childhood care and education to children \- To, guide, observe, stimulate and supervise children in a safe and caring environment. \- To assist in the planning and implementation of an age appropriate stimulating programme of activities which will enhance the child’s natural growth and development \- Ensure the safety, security and wellbeing of all children in your care. **Qualifications/ Experience:** \- Minimum QQI Level 5 in Early Childhood Education or equivalent. (All qualifications from outside Ireland can be validated by DCEDIY) \- 1 Years experience is desirable but not essential \- First Aid, Manual Handling and HACCP is an advantage but training is provided. \- Desire to learn progressively and keep up to date on current practices. **Working Hours:** Our service hours are between 8am\-5\.30pm. Our Teachers work on weekly rotated shifts, 8\-5/8\.15\-5\.15/8\.30\-5\.30 We try our best to reply to all applicants, however in instances of high volume of applications or applicants not meeting requirements. We will only be able to respond to successful candidates. Interview can take place in person or through video call. Work Location: In person Pay: €16\.50\-€17\.50 per hour Benefits: * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: front of house team member
description: **front of house team member \| full time** at wagamama, food is life. we’re fast paced, full of energy and big on creating brilliant experiences. no scripts, no awkward service, just warm welcomes, real conversations and a team that moves together from the first hello to the last bowl cleared, you’ll help make the restaurant feel alive **the role \|** as a front of house team member, you’ll be a big part of what makes every shift special. you’ll welcome guests in, bring the menu to life and help keep service flowing smoothly from start to finish it’s a role for people who love good food, great vibes and being right in the middle of the action **if you...** * love making people feel welcome and looked after * enjoy talking about food and making genuine recommendations * thrive in lively, fast moving environments * stay calm, switched on and one step ahead when it gets busy * notice the little things and care about getting them right * bring good energy and love being part of a team we’d love to hear from you **wok’s in it for you \|** * biweekly pay \+ a share of tips\* * flexible hours to fit around your life * access up to 30% of your earnings before payday with wagestream * free food on shift, yes, anything on the menu, plus 50% off outside of work * industry leading family friendly policy, with 26 weeks full pay \+ 13 weeks half pay for new parents * pangaia designed uniform, made for you to make your own * first taste of brand new menu drops before anyone else * parties, perks and plenty of opportunities to grow with us * all the good stuff too, pension, holiday, training, discounts, cycle to work scheme, discounted gym memberships \+ long service awards **apply today \+ take a seat at the bench** due to the high volume of applications we receive, we’ll send you an immersive assessment by email after you apply. please keep an eye on your inbox and complete it within 3 days we’re proud to be a disability confident employer. there’s a seat at our bench for everyone **Feed Yourself, Feed Others** * 100% of tips go to our teams. actual tips by restaurant will vary. tips are left solely at our guests’ discretion and are not considered as wages by the company nor are they guaranteed in any way
